system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";

}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";

}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;

}

mixture

    specie

        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1.0;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;

        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;

        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

vertices

    (-0.15242  0.1524   -0.005)  // 6
    (-0.15242  0.1524    0.005)  // 7

    // Block 1 - upper region above wedge

    ( 0.3048  0.09144  -0.005)  // 9 (same as vertex 2)
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   -0.005)  // 10 (same as vertex 4)
    (-0.15242  0.1524   -0.005)  // 11 (same as vertex 7)

    (-0.15242  0.3048    0.005)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 11 8)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 10 9)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 9 8)
        );
    }

    top

        faces
        (

        );
    }

    wedge

        faces
        (
            (3 11 10 2)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack

        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
            (8 9 10 11)

        );

);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoCentralFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 3 2 1) 4(8 9 10 11) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/experiment-finetuned-allrun_commands/single_knockout/knockout_allrun_commands/runs/wedge/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 39.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis>## Error Analysis

### Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Inward-pointing face normals

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 3 2 1) 4(8 9 10 11)
```

The previous fix swapped the vertex ordering to `hex (0 1 2 3 ...)` and `hex (8 9 10 11 ...)`, but now the error shows the problematic faces as `4(0 3 2 1)` and `4(8 9 10 11)`. These are the **vertical edge faces** (the side faces connecting the two z-layers), not the horizontal faces anymore. This means the new ordering made the **side faces** have inward-pointing normals.

Looking at the vertices again:
- Edge 0 connects vertex 0 to vertex 8 (from z=-0.005 to z=+0.005)
- Edge 1 connects vertex 1 to vertex 9
- Edge 2 connects vertex 2 to vertex 10
- Edge 3 connects vertex 3 to vertex 11

Face `4(0 3 2 1)` involves vertices 0,3,2,1 — these are the **bottom face** vertices. With the current ordering `hex (0 1 2 3 ...)`, the bottom face goes 0→1→2→3. Viewed from +z, this is counter-clockwise, so the normal should point **upward**. But the error says it has an inward-pointing normal. This suggests the coordinate system convention used by OpenFOAM considers the opposite.

Let me think about this systematically. In OpenFOAM, the right-hand rule applies: for vertices (i,j,k,l), the normal direction follows the right-hand rule from i→j→k. If the normal points inward, then the vertex ordering needs to be reversed.

The key insight: **face 4** refers to the face formed by following edge 4 around the block. Edge 4 connects vertex 0 to vertex 3. Following the block vertex sequence `0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11`:

- Starting at vertex 0, going to vertex 1 (along edge 0)
- Then to vertex 2 (along edge 1)  
- Then to vertex 3 (along edge 2)
- Back to vertex 0 (along edge 3)

So face 4 is actually the face formed by vertices connected via edges 0,1,2,3 — which is the **bottom face** at z=-0.005. The vertex sequence for this face is 0→1→2→3. As established, this is counter-clockwise when viewed from +z, so the normal points **upward** (+z direction). But the error says it has an inward-pointing normal.

Wait — maybe the issue is that the **top face** (connecting the two z-layers) is the one with the problem? Face 3 connects vertices 3→2→1→0 (going backward along the bottom face), which would be clockwise when viewed from +z → normal pointing **downward** (inward). And face 4 connects 8→9→10→11 (the top face), which is also clockwise when viewed from +z → normal pointing **downward** (inward).

Yes! That's it. The error lists faces `4(0 3 2 1)` and `4(8 9 10 11)`. Face index 4 corresponds to the **top face** of the block (the face at z=+0.005), which consists of vertices 8,9,10,11. The sequence given `(8 9 10 11)` is clockwise when viewed from +z → normal pointing **downward** (into the block interior). Similarly, face `4(0 3 2 1)` is the bottom face with vertices listed in reverse order (0→3→2→1), which is clockwise when viewed from +z → normal pointing **downward** (inward).

**The fix**: Reverse the vertex ordering of both blocks completely. Instead of `hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11)`, use `hex (3 2 1 0 11 10 9 8)`. This way:
- Bottom face: vertices 3→2→1→0 (counter-clockwise when viewed from +z → normal upward, outward)
- Top face: vertices 11→10→9→8 (counter-clockwise when viewed from +z → normal upward, outward)

### Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find mesh

This is purely a cascading effect — `blockMesh` failed, so no mesh exists for the solver. Once the mesh is fixed, this resolves automatically.

## Summary

**Primary Fix**: Reverse the vertex ordering in both block definitions so that all six faces have outward-pointing normals. Change:
- Block 0: `hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11)` → `hex (3 2 1 0 11 10 9 8)`
- Block 1: `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` → `hex (11 10 9 8 15 14 13 12)`

This reverses the winding direction of all faces, ensuring that the right-hand rule produces outward-pointing normals for every face of both hexahedral blocks.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Change first block definition from hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) to hex (3 2 1 0 11 10 9 8); Change second block definition from hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) to hex (11 10 9 8 15 14 13 12')]
